matches.moment: Salah enters in the 62nd minute and delivers a diagonal cross from the left that Marmoush meets with a near-post run — the ball deflects off Souttar and wrong-foots Ryan.

matches.venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens FL
matches.moment: Messi receives a short corner at the edge of the area in the 38th minute, steps inside his marker, and drives low to the keeper's right. Cape Verde's block held firm for 37 minutes before the world's greatest finisher made it count.

Ghana defend diligently for 55 minutes before James Rodríguez wins a free kick 25 yards out and clips the wall-side into a spot Ghana's keeper cannot reach — a set-piece mechanism breaking what open play could not.

verdict: Three completed results from the morning session — Spain, Portugal, and Switzerland all advancing in decisive fashion — set a commanding European tone for today's Round of 32. The day's real analytical interest lies in the evening fixtures, where the data tells a consistent story: tight margins, organized defenses, and single-goal decisions. Egypt's case against Australia rests entirely on whether Mohamed Salah can contribute meaningfully from a hamstring he is visibly nursing, and the quality-comparable data from Belgium and Iran produces a 1.0 goals/game average — a thin but real advantage that manifests as Egypt 1-0. Argentina versus Cape Verde is the clearest prediction of the day not because of certainty but because the quality differential is simply so vast that Cape Verde's two clean sheets against Spain and Saudi Arabia function as a ceiling test, not a floor test: Messi at 6 goals in 3 games with an elite xG/shot profile will find the net, and Álvarez's pressing earns a second. Colombia versus Ghana is the hardest call — Colombia's 1.63 xG against Portugal went unrewarded, James Rodríguez's free kicks are the most reliable mechanism for breaking a compact block, and Ghana's adjusted expected goals against Colombia's elite defense is simply not enough. The 1-0 Colombia call is correct but carries the lowest conviction of the three predictions. — The Analyst.

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.stat: 1 goal + 2 assists + 11 chances created in group stage (2nd most in Group G behind Trossard/Belgium)
players.watch: His movement after arriving on the pitch — the first 10 minutes of Salah time will determine whether Egypt create the decisive opening vs Australia

players.name: Lionel Messi
players.stat: 6 goals in 3 group matches; WC all-time scoring record (19 goals); scored in 7 consecutive WC matches
players.watch: First touch after receiving in the right half-space 30-40 yards from goal — his trigger position for drives to goal that Cape Verde's defense has never faced at this level

players.name: James Rodríguez
players.stat: 2014 WC Golden Boot (6 goals); elite free kick and key-pass specialist; confirmed starter vs Ghana
players.watch: Set piece delivery from 20-30 yards — any free kick in that zone is Colombia's highest-probability scoring event against Ghana's organized block

players.name: Nestory Irankunda
players.stat: 0.41 goals per 90 (tournament); youngest player to score for Australia at a World Cup
players.watch: First touch after Australia wins ball in transition — Egypt's fullbacks push high, Irankunda's pace in behind is Australia's sole genuine counter-attacking threat

players.name: Thomas Partey
players.stat: Defensive midfielder anchor; midfield screen for Ghana's back four throughout group stage
players.watch: Positioning between the lines when Colombia enter the final third — if Partey steps up and wins the ball, Ghana stay compact; if he is bypassed, Colombia create at will
